original_abstract:
This research investigates the transformative role of the "Merdeka Mengajar" (Independence in Teaching) platform in advancing teacher professional development and technology integration within the vocational education framework at SMK Negeri 1 Dlanggu Mojokerto. Utilizing a qualitative case study approach, the study probes into how the platform serves as a catalyst for educational quality by fostering modern pedagogical skills, collaborative learning environments, and the strategic use of digital assets. The findings reveal that Merdeka Mengajar provides educators with streamlined access to a rich repository of learning materials, empowering them to transition toward more interactive and innovative instructional methods that better mirror the demands of the contemporary global job market.

However, the transition is not without friction; the study identifies persistent challenges, most notably the technical hurdles associated with aligning new digital content with established curricula and the limitations of existing school infrastructure. Despite these obstacles, teachers reported a largely positive experience, noting that the platform significantly bolsters student engagement and deepens the learning process. The research highlights a critical need for sustained technical training and institutional support to fully realize the platform's potential. Ultimately, the study concludes that by adopting a strategic, collaborative approach between policymakers and practitioners, the Merdeka Mengajar platform can bridge the gap between traditional vocational schooling and the digital future, ensuring students are equipped with highly relevant, future-ready skills.
humanized_abstract:
This study aims to analyze the impact of a national Movement for Teacher Professional Development program namely “Merdeka Mengajar “(Independence in Teaching) for teacher professional development and technology use in vocational education context at SMK Negeri 1 Dlanggu Mojokerto. Based on qualitative case study, this research aims to investigate how this movement promotes educational quality through enhancing teacher’s pedagogical skills, building of collaborative learning environment and digital resources. The results show that Merdeka Mengajar programme enabled teachers to have easy access to on-line teaching resources and thus to modernize their teaching practices into more dynamic, creative and global market responsive skills which are required by the new work force in the global era.

While the move to online learning is in full swing, many barriers remain and the study uncovered many of them, particularly related to the technological barrier of getting new digital learning materials in sync with old curricula and the inadequacy of school infrastructure. Despite these challenges, teachers described the online learning platform as “very interactive” and that it was “very helpful in getting students involved in the learning process”. The study concludes that the teachers need training that is on-going to make full use of the digital learning platform and that there is a need for a more collaborative approach from Ministry of Education and policy makers towards teachers to implement the digital learning platform to the fullest potential of preparing students with the right vocational skills that are relevant and future-proof.
